races.name: Join The Johnson White Racing Syndicate Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: Falls Of Acharn
runners.spotlight: 2-4 on the Flat in Ireland; scrambled home on hurdling debut at Musselburgh (extended 1m7f) on New Year's Day; last of six in Listed race over that C&D (again good to soft) four weeks later, although he wasn't beaten far after racing too freely; tailed off in a Grade 2 one month ago but should be of major interest with his sights lowered today.
runners.comment: Tailed off in a Grade 2 one month ago; should be of major interest with his sights lowered

runners.name: Sinchi Roca
runners.spotlight: Something went wrong when favourite on handicap debut last time but previous bumper (including on good ground) and hurdles form makes him one of the leading candidates in this line-up.
runners.comment: Something went wrong on handicap debut last time; big shout on previous bumper/hurdle form

runners.name: Ettore
runners.spotlight: Well related (including as half-brother to a useful jumper) but 0-4 in these colours on Flat for Roger Varian, modest form in his closest finish; weak 11-2, 33l sixth of nine in a juvenile hurdle at Newbury (2m, good to soft) for new yard last month, looking best watched for the time being.
runners.comment: 0-4 on Flat; weak 11-2, beaten 33l in a juvenile hurdle at Newbury (2m, good to soft)
runners.owner: Exors Of The Late John Connolly

runners.name: Johnjay
runners.spotlight: Fair performer at his best in 23-race Flat career (most for Roger Teal; two runs for this yard) which includes two 1m wins; hooded on latest outing and today; probably best watched on hurdles debut.
runners.comment: Fair performer at his best in 23-race Flat career which includes two 1m wins

runners.name: Grand Harbour
runners.spotlight: 0-13 as modest maiden on the Flat, runner-up once over 1m4f; brother to a Flat/hurdles winner but a watching brief is recommended today.
runners.comment: 0-13 as modest maiden on Flat, runner-up once over 1m4f; brother to a Flat/hurdles winner

runners.name: Signore Enzo
runners.spotlight: Showed no significant promise in two Flat novice races (7f/1m) last summer; 300-1 when unruly at the start on his intended hurdle debut at Exeter last Tuesday.
runners.comment: No significant Flat promise; 300-1 when unruly before intended hurdle debut last Tuesday

runners.name: Famous Shoes
runners.spotlight: Some promise in brief Flat career but he hasn't shown much in his four runs over hurdles; had wind surgery before penultimate start.
runners.comment: Some promise in brief Flat career but he hasn't shown much in his four runs over hurdles

runners.name: Modern Style
runners.spotlight: Tailed off for Charlie Appleby on sole Flat start and has not accomplished much for Chris Down over hurdles (2m on good) at Ludlow or Kempton.
runners.comment: Tailed off for C Appleby on sole Flat start and did not do much for C Down over hurdles

races.name: Experience The Thrill With Hottotrot Racing Mares' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Lady Caro
runners.spotlight: Sole win (from 22 races) in March 2025; nearly all minor honours this term, runner-up under Cameron Johnstone-Baker over C&D (good) last time; strong each-way claims again.
runners.comment: Sole win (from 22 races) in March 2025; nearly all minor honours this term, latest C&D

runners.name: My Louise
runners.spotlight: Dual bumper winner (first two starts) who had struggled to match that level over hurdles until a close third of nine at Newton Abbot (3m2f, good) last June off 1lb higher than today; that came after wind surgery and in a first-time tongue-tie (kept since) but she was pulled up at Plumpton (2m4f, soft) when next seen four weeks ago; mixed messages.
runners.comment: Close third (3m2f, good) last June off 1lb higher after wind op; pulled up four weeks ago

runners.name: Newmill Getaway
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner who is 0-11 over hurdles but last four starts (including three minor honours) suggest she will be on the premises.
runners.comment: Bumper winner; 0-11 over hurdles but last four starts suggest she will be on the premises

runners.name: Pooleys Promise
runners.spotlight: 28-1 winner at Ffos Las (3m, good) last May when 1lb lower; ran creditably next time but heavy defeats (soft ground a possible excuse) late last year on last two runs for Ryan Potter; new yard turns to cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Heavy defeats (soft ground a possible excuse) last two runs for R Potter; cheekpieces now

runners.name: Kantagua Du Large
runners.spotlight: Chase win in France; British hurdles form after six starts (mostly over about 2m4f) does not match up to this mark, even though she's had a marked fall in the weights; cheekpieces (French win was record in them) returned last time; now has 7lb-claiming amateur on board and a new trip.
runners.comment: British hurdles form (six starts, mostly over about 2m4f) does not match up to this mark

runners.name: Jewel Eyed Judy
runners.spotlight: Won on reappearance at Leicester (2m4f, soft) in November off 4lb lower but out of form in her three starts since, with the last two over 3m2f/3m; now has headgear on for the first time.
runners.comment: Won at Leicester (2m4f, soft) in November but out of form since; headgear for first time

runners.name: Whatmakes Honey B
runners.spotlight: Experienced pointer who won a Taunton hurdle race (2m3f, good) off 1lb higher last March; returned to form when fourth over that C&D (heavy) last month but pulled up (reportedly lame) on chase debut four weeks ago; unraced beyond 2m5f under rules.
runners.comment: Won here (2m3f, good) last March; back to form penultimate start; reportedly lame latest

runners.name: Miss Tiara
runners.spotlight: Had wind surgery (her second) before handicap debut at Exeter (2m2f, good to soft; 8-1) 18 days ago but she was beaten 25l and reportedly made a respiratory noise; new trip and needs overall improvement.
runners.comment: Wind surgery before recent handicap debut (2m2f) but reportedly made a respiratory noise

runners.name: Connies Hill
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner last May but she's not achieved much since in a bumper or four hurdle races; 14-1 when fitted with cheekpieces on handicap debut (big step up to 3m2f; 14-1) on latest start; now tries blinkers and her tongue-tie is removed.
runners.comment: Not much in a bumper or four hurdle races, handicap debut latest; different headgear now

runners.name: Poets Reflection
runners.spotlight: Sole win (33 races) was on good ground here but in 2021; results over the last year have been pretty grim (latest in first-time cheekpieces) and she's now 6lb out of the handicap; unraced beyond an extended 2m5f.
runners.comment: Sole win was in 2021 and her results over the last year have been pretty grim; new trip

races.name: 4Racing Owners Club Charitable Syndicates Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Daany
runners.spotlight: Had an excellent 2024 but absent 343 days afterwards and has not regained his form in his seven starts (the first three on Flat) in 2025 and 2026.
runners.comment: Had an excellent 2024 but absent 343 days afterwards and has not regained his form
runners.owner: Duckhaven D Kenealy W Sprigg M Sprigg

runners.name: Thistle Be The One
runners.spotlight: Better in bumpers than he's been over hurdles and this season has been particularly disappointing; wore cheekpieces penultimate start and tongue-tie latest, but now it's a tongue-tie and visor.
runners.comment: This season has been particularly disappointing and he now has a change in equipment

runners.name: Man Oh Man
runners.spotlight: 0-5 in Irish points and 0-9 over hurdles; twice third in handicaps from his four British starts, however, and those better efforts were at this track, admittedly over 2m3f but he brings some appeal; wears headgear first time.
runners.comment: Twice third in handicaps from his four British starts and those 3rds were at this track

runners.name: Castle Quarter
runners.spotlight: Four-time AW Flat winner who also scored in a Uttoxeter handicap hurdle (2m, good) last May and is just 1lb higher today; third at Wincanton on penultimate start was easily the best of his four efforts this autumn/winter, so he's not easy to trust.
runners.comment: Wincanton 3rd two starts back was easily the best of his four efforts this autumn/winter
runners.owner: Mr & Mrs R Newiss & Gavin Teversham

runners.name: Saucats
runners.spotlight: 40-1 third at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in November reads well but there's been nothing resembling that since and he's now 0-18, all over hurdles.
runners.comment: 40-1 third at Wincanton (1m7f, good) in November reads well; nothing resembling that since

runners.name: Raging Al
runners.spotlight: 0-6 on Flat and 0-5 as hurdler but last two outings were his best in this sphere and dropped 2lb since last month's handicap hurdle debut; player.
runners.comment: Last two outings were his best in this sphere; down 2lb since 2nd on handicap hurdle debut

runners.name: Mi Sueno
runners.spotlight: Won two in a row on soft ground early in 2024 but right out of form since; this is his second start for current trainer.
runners.comment: Won twice on soft early in 2024 but right out of form since; second start for trainer

runners.name: St Lukes Chelsea
runners.spotlight: Remains winless after 16 races (six Flat, ten over hurdles) but the last four, latest over C&D, all suggest a major role today.
runners.comment: 0-16 but his last four starts, latest over C&D, all suggest a major role today

runners.name: Ezmerellda
runners.spotlight: Won a maiden hurdle in 2023 but very hard to fancy judged on her seven races from November 2024 onwards, the last two in cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Very hard to fancy judged on her seven races from November 2024 onwards

runners.name: Victory Shout
runners.spotlight: Pretty useful on the Flat at his best but lost his way in that scene last May/June and has made a quiet start over hurdles; 33-1 and upped to 2m5f when pulled up on handicap hurdle debut last time.
runners.comment: Quiet start over hurdles; 33-1 and upped to 2m5f when pulled up on handicap hurdle debut

runners.name: Dance Havana
runners.spotlight: Flat wins in 2022 and 2023; has had only four races over hurdles but soundly beaten in two soft-ground handicaps when last seen early in 2024.
runners.comment: Soundly beaten in two soft-ground handicaps when last seen early in 2024

runners.name: Anthracite
runners.spotlight: Modest in four Flat races (2023 and 2024) but, after a big layoff, no worthwhile form in three maiden hurdles this year; at least handicap hurdle debut should help and the market can guide again.
runners.comment: After a big layoff, no worthwhile form in three maiden hurdles; handicap hurdle debut

runners.name: Walk In The West
runners.spotlight: Lacks solid form claims after two bumpers and three hurdle races but the hood and tongue-tie are now fitted and it's his handicap debut.
runners.comment: Lacks solid form claims; hood and tongue-tie are now fitted and it's his handicap debut

races.name: Kayley Woollacott Syndicates & Blackmore Racing Club Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: Gyenyame
runners.spotlight: On a losing run but he's proving a consistent sort and came in a good second of eight in 3m1f handicap chase at Wincanton 19 days ago; he's firmly in the picture again off an unchanged mark.
runners.comment: On a losing run but good second at Wincanton latest; firmly in the picture off same mark

runners.name: Wicked Thoughts
runners.spotlight: Point/hurdles scorer who jumped well when making a winning start in this sphere over C&D in November but he's been off since coming in a remote fourth of six in 3m Exeter handicap 74 days ago; possibly worth another chance after a break.
runners.comment: C&D winner but remote fourth at Exeter since; possibly worth another chance after a break

runners.name: Morfee
runners.spotlight: Ended a losing sequence at Fontwell (3m2f) in June but his more recent form is not so encouraging, a remote third of four in 3m2f Plumpton handicap chase 29 days ago; this C&D winner needs to revive.
runners.comment: Remote third at Plumpton 29 days ago; this C&D winner needs to revive

runners.name: A Definite Getaway
runners.spotlight: A dual winner (at about 3m) last April but he's not been seen since coming in a well-held third of five in 3m2f handicap chase at Newton Abbot ten months ago; he needs to hit the ground running.
runners.comment: Off since a well-held third at Newton Abbot ten months ago; needs to hit ground running

runners.name: Good Friday Fairy
runners.spotlight: Resumed with victory at Exeter (2m3f) in November and in good form until pulled up in 2m3f handicap chase at Fontwell ten days ago; this course winner is the sort to bounce back in a change of headgear, though.
runners.comment: Pulled up at Fontwell ten days ago; this course winner is the sort to bounce back though

runners.name: Edeiffs Rock
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced this term but she looked better than ever last time when gaining a second C&D success, cosily making all; hiked up 10lb but she's a big player.
runners.comment: Last time cosily gained a second C&D success; hiked up 10lb but she's a big player

races.name: Racehorse Ownership From Â£25 At racingclub.com Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Knock Off Soxs
runners.spotlight: Got off the mark in 2m novice here in December and advanced his form on his handicap debut when third of ten at Exeter (2m) 18 days ago; he must enter calculations eased 1lb for his in-form yard.
runners.comment: Advanced form on h'cap debut at Exeter latest; must enter calculations eased 1lb

runners.name: Treasure Planet
runners.spotlight: A fair ex-Irish Flat maiden who got off the mark on just his second start over hurdles at Ludlow (2m) in November; went too freely next time but hooded when a very good second of six on his handicap debut at Newbury (2m) 24 days ago; a player on that form but headgear is left off now over this longer trip.
runners.comment: Hooded when very good Newbury second latest; headgear off now and has stamina to prove

runners.name: Siam Park
runners.spotlight: Gained most recent chase win at Plumpton (2m1f) in November and he posted a solid fourth of ten back hurdling in 2m handicap here 15 days ago; he ought to be thereabouts off the same mark.
runners.comment: Solid fourth back hurdling here 15 days ago; he ought to be thereabouts off the same mark

runners.name: Mammies Boy
runners.spotlight: Scored at Warwick (2m5f) on his final run for Jonjo and AJ O'Neill but he looked rusty after ten months off when last of 15 in 2m4f Wincanton handicap for new yard 27 days ago; this C&D winner should take a step forward here with cheekpieces added.
runners.comment: Rusty for new yard after ten months off when last at Wincanton; should take a step forward

runners.name: Il Va De Soi
runners.spotlight: Winless since April 2024 and he arrives out of sorts, a well-held seventh in 2m5f Warwick handicap hurdle 16 days ago; he's not easy to make a case for.
runners.comment: Winless since April 2024 and he arrives out of sorts; he's not easy to make a case for

runners.name: Wolf Walker
runners.spotlight: Point scorer who showed fairly useful form when runner-up on his first three starts over hurdles; pulled up on his handicap debut at Sandown (2m4f) 17 days ago though so needs to bounce back.
runners.comment: Runner-up first three starts but pulled up on Sandown h'cap debut 17 days ago; more needed
runners.owner: Robert Fear And Mrs Tracey Ham

runners.name: Khafre
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner who has progressed with each of his three runs in this sphere, comfortably landing novice over C&D last month; big shout with better still to come now going into handicaps.
runners.comment: Improving sort who readily landed C&D novice latest; big shout now going into h'caps

races.name: Crest Racing Mares' Handicap Chase (Queen Boudicca Mares' Chase Series Qualifier)

runners.name: Bobbis Beauty
runners.spotlight: Course winner who was in good form until a below-par fifth of six in 2m Warwick handicap chase 16 days ago; cheekpieces go on now and she's the sort to bounce back.
runners.comment: Course winner who was in good form until fifth at Warwick latest; the sort to bounce back

runners.name: Ryders Rock
runners.spotlight: Made a winning chasing debut at Newton Abbot (2m5f) in May; failed to stay 3m1f when fourth there subsequently but shaped encouragingly reverted to hurdles after seven months off when third in 2m2f Exeter handicap 18 days ago; merits serious consideration back in this sphere.
runners.comment: Encouraging hurdles third at Exeter latest; merits serious consideration back chasing

runners.name: Miss Fedora
runners.spotlight: A fair 2m1f chase winner at her best but she refused at the last fence in 3m5f handicap chase at Fakenham on her final run for Seamus Mullins last month; tongue tied for her new yard with something to prove.
runners.comment: Refused last at Fakenham final run for Seamus Mullins; something to prove for new yard

runners.name: OGradys Hill
runners.spotlight: Points scorer but she failed to win over hurdles for Harry Derham and has offered little in a trio of runs sent over fences for her current yard, a well-held fifth of eight in 2m Ludlow handicap last month; others are preferred.
runners.comment: Offered little in a trio of runs over fences for current yard; others are preferred

runners.name: Prairie Queen
runners.spotlight: Is 0-15 over hurdles but she got back on track in first-time cheekpieces when third of 11 in 2m4f handicap at Hereford 17 days ago; she's much respected on her first go over fences with the headgear retained.
runners.comment: Is 0-15 over hurdles but good Hereford third latest; much respected on debut over fences

runners.name: Fox Wallace
runners.spotlight: Gained a breakthrough victory over fences at Stratford (2m1f) last May but her more recent form is far less encouraging, pulled up over C&D in December; she needs to take a major step forward after a break.
runners.comment: Pulled up over C&D in December; she needs to take a major step forward after a break
runners.owner: M Enticott / Ella Pickard Racing Club

races.name: South West Rail Solutions Open National Hunt Flat Race (Category 1 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: Artaban
runners.spotlight: 60,000euros 3yo; Motivator gelding; half-brother to point winner Happy Day Emery; dam is an unraced half-sister to useful French 2m1f/2m2f chase winner Groove, out of a sister to French 2m6f Grade 2 chase winner Farlow Des Mottes; yard has taken this twice in past three years so he rates an interesting newcomer.
runners.comment: Motivator gelding; yard has taken this twice in past three years so interesting newcomer

runners.name: Crest Of Arms
runners.spotlight: Son of Passing Glance who has shaped with promise on both his runs, set a lot to do when seventh of 16 in Cheltenham Listed bumper on New Year's Day; tongue tie added now; the clear form pick.
runners.comment: Promising seventh of 16 in Cheltenham Listed bumper on New Year's Day; the clear form pick

runners.name: Roxy Rocks
runners.spotlight: 25,000euros foal, 35,000euros 3yo; half-sister to bumper winner Shantewe; dam, unraced half-sister to 7f 2yo and useful 2m-2m6f hurdle/chase winner Chief Yeoman, is out of a half-sister to US 1m2f Grade 1 winner Unknown Quantity; the market can guide for this daughter of Jukebox Jury.
runners.comment: The market can guide for this daughter of Jukebox Jury on her debut outing here

runners.name: Arms Park
runners.spotlight: Fair form shown when placed in a pair of bumpers, leading long way when third of 11 over C&D in November; this son of Walk In The Park must enter calculations after a break.
runners.comment: Good third over C&D in November; this son of Walk In The Park must enter calculations

runners.name: Law Of The Jungle
runners.spotlight: Hooded and tongue tied when a well-held debut eighth in 1m5f bumper at Saint-Jean-de-Monts 19 months ago; has her fitness to prove starting out for a new yard after a long absence.
runners.comment: Well held in bumper at Saint-Jean-de-Monts 19 months ago; fitness to prove for new yard

runners.name: Barachiel
runners.spotlight: Well held in a pair of bumpers, beating one at Haydock when last seen out ten months ago; needs subsequent wind surgery to spark improvement.
runners.comment: Beat one in Haydock bumper ten months ago; needs subsequent wind op to spark improvement

races.name: Ladies Day Sunday 16th August Handicap Chase (GBB Race)

runners.name: I Am Rocco
runners.spotlight: Running well until blowing out on his final run in November but remains a maiden and he's debuting for a new yard after 131 days off.
runners.comment: Remains a maiden and he's debuting for a new yard after 131 days off

runners.name: Lilting Verse
runners.spotlight: Hasn't been at her best of late (behind Smurfette latest) but she has the form to feature off this mark and won't mind getting back on drier ground; stays this far; possible.
runners.comment: Has the form to feature off this mark and won't mind getting back on drier ground

runners.name: Smurfette
runners.spotlight: Consistent 8yo who made it 2-2 here when a narrow winner earlier this month; only raised 3lb and this willing mare should have every chance; has won on good.
runners.comment: Consistent mare who made it 2-2 here when a narrow winner earlier this month

runners.name: Icaque de LIsle
runners.spotlight: Overall chase record of 2-16; dug deep to win over a trip just shy of 3m at Lingfield (soft; 5lb lower) and 3m6f on heavy found him out at Exeter a month ago; may prefer softer ground in an ideal world but hard to rule out.
runners.comment: 3m6f on heavy too much for him last time; does enjoy some cut underfoot, however

runners.name: Copshill Lad
runners.spotlight: Defied an even longer break at Hereford in November (3m1f, good) off only 1lb lower and his following run was just as good; could easily leave his latest below-par run behind.
runners.comment: Won after a longer break in November and could leave his latest below-par run behind

races.name: Book Your Party At Southwell Racecourse Novices' Handicap Chase

runners.name: Shadowfax Of Rohan
runners.spotlight: Won on his chase/stable debut at Catterick (by 4.5l; 1m7f, good to soft) and bumped into a big improver back there 20 days ago (on good); seriously considered off the same mark.
runners.comment: Won on his chase/stable debut at Catterick and bumped into a big improver there next time
runners.owner: K Jardine R Griffiths W Duffus

runners.name: Catchim
runners.spotlight: Front runner who has had a break since a poor final run; that did extend his losing sequence over fences to ten but he's threatened on occasions, as when second here in November off 2lb higher; trip/ground fine.
runners.comment: 0-10 as a chaser but has threatened, as when second here in November off 2lb higher

runners.name: Classical Sting
runners.spotlight: Made a winning handicap/chasing debut at Kelso in November (2m1f, soft) and was then second of four on good ground; it was heavy when he pulled up and then, with a hood replaced by cheekpieces, he got back on track with another second over hurdles at this track; should be involved.
runners.comment: Has been in good form over hurdles and fences; should be involved

runners.name: Known Warrior
runners.spotlight: Lightly raced maiden; two good runs after wind surgery, especially last time when going down by only 2l at Plumpton (2m1f, soft); ran well in a bumper on this sort of ground.
runners.comment: Two good runs after wind surgery, especially last time when going down by only 2l

runners.name: Ferando
runners.spotlight: Can't make much of a case on his form but he is very lightly raced and was noticeably short in the betting (100-30) when well held on last week's stable debut at Ffos Las (2m3f, soft; second chase run); perhaps he'll enjoy this drier surface.
runners.comment: Poor form claims but performed below market expectations on recent yard debut

races.name: Southwell Open Maiden National Hunt Flat Race (Category 3 Elimination) (GBB Race)

runners.name: LAmalric
runners.spotlight: Failed to complete in four Irish points, though was in contention when falling in the penultimate one; Olly Murphy clearly saw something he liked to have taken him on board.
runners.comment: Failed to complete in four Irish points but has joined a leading yard for rules action

runners.name: Tandys Bridge
runners.spotlight: The form of his second on yielding to soft in Ireland worked out well and, although most disappointing on yard debut at Warwick (good to soft), he did go off the 2-1 favourite; interesting to see if supporters return.
runners.comment: Irish run reads well; tailed off at Warwick but was a strong 2-1 market leader

runners.name: Music Song
runners.spotlight: 5-1 at Doncaster and only faded out of the picture late on (soft); may approve of these drier conditions and stable is always respected in bumpers.
runners.comment: 5-1 at Doncaster and only faded out of the picture late on (soft)

runners.name: Indulto Rouge
runners.spotlight: Won a fast-ground Irish point by 18l; tongue tied for both his bumpers; a keeping-on third of 14 at Hereford (good) and has had wind surgery since finishing well beaten on soft at Ascot (behind Champion Bumper third Bass Hunter); of definite interest back at this level and on ground that he clearly handles.
runners.comment: Soft ground perhaps not ideal last time, and that was at Ascot; had wind op

runners.name: Anchor
runners.spotlight: Excellent Flat pedigree as a Juddmonte-bred son of Kingman; however, he was sold for 3,000gns as a 3yo and can only be watched with no experience on the board.
runners.comment: Excellent Flat pedigree as a Juddmonte-bred son of Kingman but sold on for 3,000gns

runners.name: Telepieces
runners.spotlight: Easy to back and tailed off in her first two bumpers, on good to soft and good.
runners.comment: Easy to back and tailed off in her first two bumpers, on good to soft and good

runners.name: Scotts Legacy
runners.spotlight: Folded late on and beaten about 20l on soft ground at Hereford; however, he did go off a short-priced favourite and encounters different conditions here.
runners.comment: Beaten 20l at Hereford but he was a short-priced favourite and the ground was soft

races.name: Free Bets On attheraces.com Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Raby Mere
runners.spotlight: Two wins last year for Olly Murphy and those were on good ground; beaten 28l and 26l in two runs for current yard, though conditions were soft and good to soft; better can be expected here, though all his notable form is at about 2m4f.
runners.comment: Well beaten in two runs for current yard but this good ground will suit him better
runners.owner: C J Underwood And Caroline Fryer

runners.name: Jack The Savage
runners.spotlight: Had dropped to a good mark and finally exploited it with a head success at Hereford (3m1f, good); that was a weak race but he's only 2lb higher and should give some less exposed rivals plenty to think about.
runners.comment: Had dropped to a good mark and finally exploited it with a head success at Hereford
runners.owner: M White J Frampton L Horsfall

runners.name: Peckforton Hills
runners.spotlight: Bumper winner; beaten 5l on handicap debut at Sedgefield (2m4f, good to soft; first-time cheekpieces) but more than double that margin when behind an easy winner at Doncaster (2m3f; tongue-tie added); lightly raced so retains potential and he's up in trip.
runners.comment: Placed in both handicaps; lightly raced so retains potential and he's up in trip

runners.name: Broderick
runners.spotlight: He was lucky to win his maiden 14 months ago and is now 0-5 in handicaps after pulling up at Newcastle (2m7f, good to soft); others have more going for them.
runners.comment: He was lucky to win his maiden 14 months ago and is now 0-5 in handicaps

runners.name: Big Bert
runners.spotlight: Hasn't really progressed as expected from his competitive bumper runs and pulled up in his latest maiden after hanging left handed around Hereford; this track may be more to his liking and this is his first handicap in a new tongue-tie.
runners.comment: Not progressing but this is his first handicap in a new tongue-tie

runners.name: Redeeming Love
runners.spotlight: It's been a struggle in novice races but she now goes into a handicap with a tongue-tie fitted and over a much longer trip that could benefit according to her pedigree; would merit greater attention if at all strong in the betting.
runners.comment: Tongue-tie on for handicap debut and would merit attention if at all strong in the betting
runners.owner: R & W The 8 Amigos Burns Austerfield

runners.name: Honneur De Sivola
runners.spotlight: A long time since he showed his best form and very opposable on how he's performed in the last year, especially as he's out of the weights.
runners.comment: 0-18 under rules and very opposable on how he's performed in the last year

races.name: Easter Sunday Family Fun Day Novices' Hurdle (GBB Race)

runners.name: The Flier Begley
runners.spotlight: Six-time chase winner who won last summer but twice disappointed since; he's 0-8 as a hurdler but ability needs respecting when there's no depth to this race.
runners.comment: All his wins have been over fences but he's back hurdling in a race with no depth

runners.name: Hillberry Hill
runners.spotlight: Contested two maiden hurdles in Ireland and prevailed narrowly at Thurles (2m4f, yielding); that form has some substance and he later changed hands for Â£70,000; has been found a weak race to carry his penalty.
runners.comment: Irish maiden hurdle winner who has been found a weak race to carry his penalty

runners.name: Sea Of Fortune
runners.spotlight: Also tongue tied in his bumper but he finished tailed off on good to soft at Chepstow; stable second string here by the looks.
runners.comment: Also tongue tied in his bumper but he finished tailed off on good to soft at Chepstow
runners.owner: Mrs I C Sellars And Miss D C Sellars

runners.name: Karlita Desbois
runners.spotlight: Her best run was the first when beaten 8l in a Uttoxeter bumper; opposable on subsequent exploits.
runners.comment: Her best run was the first and opposable on subsequent exploits

runners.name: Danger Nap
runners.spotlight: Started off well as he was second on bumper debut and then beaten only 6l in a Listed event; however, now has a regressive profile and needs to get back on track.
runners.comment: Career started promisingly but has regressed and now has to get back on track

runners.name: Tombereau
runners.spotlight: He was 150-1 when a remote fourth over C&D (soft) a month ago and is again up against it.
runners.comment: He was 150-1 when a remote fourth over C&D (soft) a month ago and is again up against it

races.name: Join Southwell Golf Club Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Sutherland
runners.spotlight: Placed in a maiden here and again on handicap debut at Newbury (2m) despite making some mistakes; a combination of this longer trip and first-time headgear could help him in that respect; can go well.
runners.comment: Placed on handicap debut at Newbury (2m) despite making some mistakes

runners.name: Moab
runners.spotlight: No match for the winner in his last two races over hurdles but both were well handicapped and returning to a longer trip looks the right move; on the shortlist.
runners.comment: No match for the winner in his last two races over hurdles but both were well handicapped

runners.name: Fine Casting
runners.spotlight: Good and bad since returned to fences (1P4); runs off a lower hurdles mark but he is a 10yo and without a win in this discipline since 2022.
runners.comment: Has been chasing; lower hurdle mark but without a win in this discipline since 2022

runners.name: Karton Plein
runners.spotlight: Won his maiden here 13 months ago when making all; twice placed in four subsequent handicaps but does look a tad high in the ratings.
runners.comment: Course maiden winner; twice placed in handicaps but looks a tad high in the ratings

runners.name: Heard That
runners.spotlight: Did well last season with three wins over fences but arrives here in no form and the return to hurdles did nothing last time.
runners.comment: Arrives here in no form and the return to hurdles did nothing last time

runners.name: Isle Of Sark
runners.spotlight: Never got involved when eighth of 11 on his stable debut at Southwell (1m4f, Tapeta) a month ago; dual hurdle winner but didn't sign off for previous yard in much form, over hurdles or fences.
runners.comment: Lost his way for previous yard; well beaten on the AW on first run for this one

runners.name: Codetalker
runners.spotlight: His only win came over Fakenham's sharp 2m but his rallying third in first-time cheekpieces at Warwick last time (2m) suggested he probably needs further now; considered.
runners.comment: Rallying third in new cheekpieces at Warwick (2m) and he now returns to further

runners.name: Purple Owl
runners.spotlight: Irish point winner; only 13l off the Supreme fourth Baron Noir in a Uttoxeter maiden before finishing tailed off on his handicap debut (both those runs on soft and in a new tongue-tie); could be interesting on the back of a wind operation.
runners.comment: His final maiden run reads well; pulled up on handicap debut but has had a wind op

runners.name: Nazare
runners.spotlight: Back-to-back wins at this sort of trip in November on good to soft; fell early when favourite next time and it was his first run on soft when tailed off at Huntingdon; on this ground and with cheekpieces added, he's one to consider.
runners.comment: Two wins in November; appeared to find soft ground against him when tailed off latest

runners.name: Menaggio
runners.spotlight: Won a novice hurdle and two handicap chases for Alan King; no closer than 13l in five runs for this yard, the last two over hurdles; had a wind operation.
runners.comment: No closer than 13l in five runs for this yard, the last two over hurdles

races.name: Visit The Bistro In Seasons Restaurant Novices' Limited Handicap Hurdle

runners.name: Dartmouth Rose
runners.spotlight: Fairly consistent in a good few handicaps until running below par last time when 3m1f shouldn't have been an issue, though good to soft might have been (seems best on good); wouldn't rule out in the new cheekpieces.
runners.comment: Capable off this mark; should be happier back down in distance and on this drier ground

runners.name: My Fortune
runners.spotlight: Struggled in novice races over shorter and was expected to improve for 2m5f on handicap debut only to find soft ground against him; remains unexposed and wears a first-time tongue-tie; could easily have a major say.
runners.comment: Soft ground to blame when raised in trip on handicap debut; can be given another chance

runners.name: Bravethewaves
runners.spotlight: None too consistent but he made it 4-12 over fences when scoring by 20l at Doncaster (2m3f, good; was left clear late on) and he's only 4lb higher for this return to hurdles; still comes with risks given overall profile and he's 0-9 in this discipline.
runners.comment: Won his final chase start by some 20l and only 4lb higher for this return to hurdling

runners.name: Kylenoe Dancer
runners.spotlight: Second run back from an absence of 679 days when the decisive winner of a Fontwell handicap (2m3f, good) in May of last year; now back from another layoff but probably has it in her to cope with the 6lb higher mark.
runners.comment: A winner last May (final run) and probably has it in her to cope with the 6lb higher mark

runners.name: Fine As Wine
runners.spotlight: Latest fifth in a Bangor maiden for mares was her best run yet under rules and gives her some momentum ahead of this handicap debut; did show plenty of promise in Irish points.
runners.comment: Latest fifth in a Bangor maiden for mares was her best run yet under rules

runners.name: Feet On The Ground
runners.spotlight: Beaten a length over C&D (good to soft) in December but hard to trust after two heavier defeats (first-time tongue-tie last time); bit risky.
runners.comment: Second here in December but hard to trust after two heavier defeats

runners.name: We Got Your Back
runners.spotlight: Has only won over fences (three times) and still held some sort of chance when unseating nine days ago at Market Rasen; his hurdle form isn't so convincing, though.
runners.comment: Capable chaser (three-time winner) but his hurdle form is not so convincing

runners.name: Troop Ship
runners.spotlight: Low mileage for an 8yo; beaten 23l (2m4f) and 13l (2m) in his two handicaps and now goes in a tongue-tie which clearly needs to make some difference.
runners.comment: Beaten 23l (2m4f) and 13l (2m) in his two handicaps and now goes in a tongue-tie

runners.name: Cher Monsieur
runners.spotlight: Game in winning at the eighth attempt at Newcastle (2m4f, good to soft; 11-4 favourite) two months ago; subsequent 4lb rise looks fair and cheekpieces go on (has twice worn a hood in the past); should be thereabouts.
runners.comment: A game winner two months ago at this trip and thereabouts off just 4lb higher

runners.name: Ever Ready
runners.spotlight: No wins in nine; best run was here on her penultimate start when going down narrowly over this trip on soft ground; appeared to get outpaced on good ground at Huntingdon 13 days ago so that's a worry.
runners.comment: Best run was here on soft ground; appeared to get outpaced last time on a good surface

runners.name: Little Big Kev
runners.spotlight: Looked a non-stayer over 2m7f on his handicap debut but was beaten even further back over 2m4f at Market Rasen (14l off the winner); off the same mark here but has undergone a wind operation, which may help.
runners.comment: Beaten 14l off this mark last time but a subsequent wind operation could help

runners.name: Cool Master
runners.spotlight: Tailed off in three 2m3f novice/maiden races but could be seen in a different light now handicapping and there's potential in his pedigree.
runners.comment: Modest so far but makes handicap debut and there's potential in the pedigree

runners.name: Keepkalmandkarryon
runners.spotlight: Forgettable efforts in his qualifying races (2m4f/2m5f) and makes handicap debut after wind surgery and a long absence; would need to see some confidence behind him.
runners.comment: Lightly raced but makes handicap debut after wind surgery and a long absence
runners.owner: DL Adams JA Adams & G McPherson

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Handicap (Div I)

runners.name: Lapidarist
runners.spotlight: Seems the most exposed contender in this field, being 0-11, but ran well (close second) in 9.5f handicap at Wolverhampton most recently and holds leading form claims back down in trip.
runners.comment: 0-11 but ran well (close second) at Wolverhampton most recently; leading form claims

runners.name: Quick Turn
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden whose handicap form features a couple of fourth-place finishes, latest at Southwell (1m); again has frame possibilities.
runners.comment: Handicap form features a couple of fourth-place finishes; frame possibilities

runners.name: Laser Luck
runners.spotlight: Midfield when 40-1 shot in similar event over C&D last week, shaping as if further may prove ideal (pedigree backs up that impression).
runners.comment: Midfield over C&D last week, shaping as if further may prove ideal

runners.name: Lady Of Clover
runners.spotlight: Thrice-raced filly who lacks concrete form claims but is open to improvement now handicapping and upped in distance; interesting, especially if the betting speaks favourably.
runners.comment: Open to improvement now handicapping and upped in distance; interesting

runners.name: Musical Soldier
runners.spotlight: Ran encouragingly in most recent 6f attempt but seemed a non-stayer over 1m kept to Kempton since; not certain to get home.
runners.comment: Seemed a non-stayer upped to 1m last time; not sure to get home

runners.name: Lady Birgma
runners.spotlight: Eight-race maiden who holds poor claims on most of her form.
runners.comment: Poor claims on form

runners.name: Alices Influence
runners.spotlight: Soundly beaten in C&D maiden on final 2yo start but this switch to handicap level gives her a more realistic chance.
runners.comment: Switch to handicap level gives her a more realistic chance

runners.name: Karmacy
runners.spotlight: Down the field in autumn starts but may be capable of a bigger effort now handicapping on reappearance; check the market signals.
runners.comment: May be capable of a bigger effort now handicapping on reappearance

runners.name: Worlington
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on novice/maiden form (7f) but this Boughey runner looks the type to improve in handicaps; gelded since last run; market support would heighten interest.
runners.comment: Boughey runner who is the type to improve in handicaps; market support should be heeded

races.name: Daily Profit Boosts At betmgm.co.uk Handicap (Div II)

runners.name: Lexington Express
runners.spotlight: Quite an exposed sort, being 0-11; otherwise one of the main players on her best form, which includes a placed effort over C&D on penultimate start (stable debut).
runners.comment: Quite exposed but is one of the main players on her best form

runners.name: Angry Ant
runners.spotlight: Seven-race maiden; did well to finish fifth in C&D handicap most recently, having come from a difficult position and endured a wide trip; possibilities if building on that effort.
runners.comment: Did well to finish fifth over C&D last time; possibilities if building on that effort

runners.name: Sorted
runners.spotlight: Went very close in similar event over C&D last week on only his second handicap attempt, building on reappearance effort (7f); good chance off same mark, assuming the form is backed up.
runners.comment: Went close in similar event over C&D last week; good chance off same mark

runners.name: Regal And Real
runners.spotlight: Has weak form claims; always towards rear over C&D on handicap/seasonal debut; addition of visor needs to make a difference.
runners.comment: Addition of visor needs to make a difference

runners.name: Buckland Belle
runners.spotlight: Six-race maiden whose 6f handicap efforts, latest at Wolverhampton, suggest she may well benefit from this step back up in distance; pedigree suggests as much; improvement possible.
runners.comment: May well benefit from this step back up in distance; improvement possible

runners.name: Warm Waters
runners.spotlight: Has shown some promise over 7f in last two starts, latest in difficult circumstances (wide trip) at Chelmsford on handicap debut; nicely bred and may do better still.
runners.comment: Has shown some promise in last two starts and may do better still

runners.name: Nautical Sky
runners.spotlight: Has looked rather short of pace in 6f maidens, modest fourth at Wolverhampton on penultimate; open to improvement over this longer trip on handicap debut; interesting.
runners.comment: Open to improvement upped in trip on handicap debut; interesting

runners.name: Cape Toronada
runners.spotlight: Far from solid on her 2yo form over 6f but may do better now handicapping and upped in trip; check the betting for further guidance.
runners.comment: May do better now handicapping and upped in trip

races.name: Join The Midnite Movement Handicap

runners.name: Colors Of Freedom
runners.spotlight: She has been banging at the door all winter and her latest C&D third (behind Kento and Henery Hawk) was another solid effort; unlikely to be too far away.
runners.comment: Banging at the door all winter; third behind two of today's rivals over C&D 17 days ago

runners.name: Kipp Kelly
runners.spotlight: Only 1lb higher than when beating Colors Of Freedom over C&D last month; solid placed efforts on his last two starts (caught wide over C&D latest) and he should remain competitive.
runners.comment: C&D win last month; solid runs in defeat on his last two starts; should go well once again

runners.name: Henery Hawk
runners.spotlight: Arrives on the back of two solid placed efforts, latterly finishing his race off well behind Kento 17 days ago; can get it wrong at the start so he will need luck in running from the inside stall.
runners.comment: Chased home Kento over C&D 17 days ago; can miss the start so stall 1 may be no advantage

runners.name: Kento
runners.spotlight: Both his wins have come in small fields over C&D, the latest when having a few of today's rivals in arrears 17 days ago; beaten favourite in a C&D classified event last week but still ran with credit; wide stall not ideal.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins to his name, the latest 17 days ago; drawn wide but he can't be discounted

runners.name: Washington Heir
runners.spotlight: He was having just the fourth start of his career when fifth of seven behind reopposing stablemate Kento over C&D 17 days ago; hood added today; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: 5th of 7 behind reopposing stablemate Kento on recent handicap debut; capable of better

runners.name: Isla Bella
runners.spotlight: Effective over C&D but recent efforts suggest she has her work cut out to defy the widest stall; new headgear tried today.
runners.comment: Conditions no problem but drawn widest and recent efforts need bettering; new headgear on

runners.name: Bluebells Boy
runners.spotlight: Low-key stable debut at Lingfield four weeks ago; should leave that behind in time but others arrive with less to prove.
runners.comment: Low-key stable debut four weeks ago; type to bounce back soon but others still look safer

runners.name: My Genghis
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap last month but twice a beaten favourite in classified events here shortly afterwards; had a short break since but he has more to do off his revised mark; one of four runners for Tony Carroll.
runners.comment: C&D handicap win last month; twice beaten in classified company soon after; up in weights

runners.name: Buraback
runners.spotlight: Conditions to suit and he ran well in defeat at Chelmsford (5f) in February/March; didn't run badly in a stronger C&D handicap last week and he can feature prominently back into 0-55 company.
runners.comment: 2nd twice at Chelmsford before last week's midfield finish here; contender down in grade

races.name: Create Your Bet Builders At Midnite Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Montevetro
runners.spotlight: Second of five in a steadily-run 1m2f maiden at Chelmsford 17 days ago (13-8 second favourite); this 800,000gns yearling is open to improvement and holds obvious claims.
runners.comment: Second of five in steadily-run 1m2f maiden 17 days ago; bred to do much better

runners.name: Royal Standard
runners.spotlight: Built on his debut effort (7f, good) when keeping on for fourth in a useful 1m maiden at Yarmouth last September; today's extra yardage should be in his favour and he's a solid contender.
runners.comment: Good 4th at Yarmouth when last seen in September (1m); new trip a plus; contender

runners.name: Cribbins
runners.spotlight: Poor form in two bumpers and a 1m4f AW maiden.
runners.comment: Poor form in two bumpers and a 1m4f AW maiden

runners.name: Stepanov
runners.spotlight: Sent off the 11-4 favourite for his Newcastle debut (7f) three months ago but he dropped away having raced freely; bred to stay this far; presumably felt capable of much better.
runners.comment: Keen/green when down the field on debut (7f) three months ago but was sent off favourite

races.name: Midnite, Building The Future Of Betting Classified Stakes (Div I)

runners.name: Top Star
runners.spotlight: Nine-race maiden; ran well for second at Southwell (5f) last month but he failed to back it up at Chelmsford 17 days ago; more to prove at 6f.
runners.comment: 0-9; ran well for second at Southwell (5f) last month but couldn't build on it last time

runners.name: Flickas Girl
runners.spotlight: Made most to win a 5f classified race at Chelmsford in January and she has held her form in defeat since, including over this trip; unlikely to be too far away.
runners.comment: Arrives in form; 0-9 over 6f but recent efforts at the distance have been creditable

runners.name: Mister McGregor
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden; he finished second over C&D three weeks ago on his first attempt in 0-50 company and he won't be far away if able to back that performance up.
runners.comment: 0-21 but he ran a cracker dropped to this grade three weeks ago; in the mix with a repeat

runners.name: Autumn Angel
runners.spotlight: Over 14 months since her last success and she has been coming up short in this level as well as in handicaps; has the ability to play a part and Rossa Ryan is a good booking; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Not found her best this winter but still capable of a prominent showing at this level

runners.name: Optimistic
runners.spotlight: Improving with racing, beaten a head into second in a three-way photo finish (Alyara fourth) to a similar event over C&D three weeks ago; still has time to do better.
runners.comment: Just missed out in a similar C&D event three weeks ago; capable of better

runners.name: She Went Whoosh
runners.spotlight: She ran well when second to the thriving Beauzon over C&D in January but little to shout about in three runs since; drawn wide and others have more compelling claims.
runners.comment: Good 2nd over C&D in January but she has failed to back it up since; wide stall tonight

runners.name: Top Biller
runners.spotlight: Front-runner; not found his best form in two runs back from a break and unlikely to enjoy an uncontested lead in this field.
runners.comment: Not fired in two runs after a break; capable at this level but an easy lead is unlikely

runners.name: Beneficiary
runners.spotlight: He has finished second in three of his last four starts and dropping back in to classified company is in his favour today; should go well.
runners.comment: Runner-up in three of his last four starts; solid candidate back in a classified event

runners.name: Alyara
runners.spotlight: Reinvented as a sprinter in recent weeks, running a solid race when fourth over C&D three weeks ago; well beaten last time though and others have more pressing claims.
runners.comment: Close fourth over C&D three weeks ago but she took a backwards step last time
runners.owner: Lynn Cullimore Pete Ponting Joe Ponting

runners.name: Gimmieminnie
runners.spotlight: 0-15 in Ireland but she finished second of 17 in a Navan handicap (6f, good) in October; former stable have gone 490 days without a winner and interesting to see how she fares in the market starting out for Daragh Bourke (sold 2,600gns in January); no tongue-tie today but the hood returns.
runners.comment: Inconsistent maiden in Ireland but has one piece of form that makes her of some interest

runners.name: I Was Thinking
runners.spotlight: Shaped quite well on his handicap debut here (7f) last October; didn't build on it at Lingfield two weeks later (1m) and now returns from 140 days off; drop to 6f not sure to suit but he is still worth a market check.
runners.comment: Drops back in trip for his seasonal return; unexposed and he needs a market check

races.name: Midnite, Building The Future Of Betting Classified Stakes (Div II)

runners.name: Dr Ali
runners.spotlight: Successful at Kempton in January; below par at Southwell last month but he bounced back to form when beaten a neck into second over C&D two weeks ago; should go well.
runners.comment: Kempton win in January and beaten a neck into second over C&D last time; high on the list

runners.name: Black Cab
runners.spotlight: C&D win for Jamie Osborne last April; slow start for current stable but he returned to form after a four-month break when second in a similar C&D event two weeks ago; solid claims if in the same form this time.
runners.comment: C&D winner; good 2nd here two weeks ago and he's a leading contender on that evidence

runners.name: Port Hedland
runners.spotlight: Beaten under 2l here in his two runs this month; hard to win with but likely to give his running again.
runners.comment: Beaten under 2l here in two runs this month; hard to win with but should run his race

runners.name: Walking On Clouds
runners.spotlight: Won a C&D handicap in December 2024, recording an RPR of 71 in the process; unfancied and has finished down the field in four runs for Chelsea Banham this winter; eligible for this 0-50 classified as a result; a return to form is likely to be foretold by the betting.
runners.comment: Struggling this winter but dropped quickly down weights; betting should tell the tale

runners.name: My Mate Beattie
runners.spotlight: 21-race maiden; two low-key runs for current stable sandwich a fair sixth here in February (7f); others bring stronger claims.
runners.comment: Exposed maiden who ran poorly here four weeks ago (7f); others have stronger claims

runners.name: Tommytwohoots
runners.spotlight: Infrequent winner but two of his three successes have come over C&D; fair effort at Chelmsford when last seen eight weeks ago; well drawn and he is more appealing than many starting out for John Mackie.
runners.comment: Two C&D wins to his name; solid effort when last seen; well drawn for stable debut; chance

runners.name: Bernard Spierpoint
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner; good runs few and far between in 2025 and he made a low-key return to action over C&D two weeks ago; revival not out of the question but others look safer.
runners.comment: Nine-time winner but 2025 was a struggle and his recent return was a low-key one

runners.name: Lynwood Lad
runners.spotlight: Capable at this level but he hasn't been firing on all cylinders in 2026; has won over C&D, and when ridden by Paddy Bradley; market can guide.
runners.comment: C&D winner; quiet so far this year; revival may be predicted by the betting

runners.name: Stroxx
runners.spotlight: Placed at Newcastle in January (5f) but no progress in three runs since; has a rare outing over 6f tonight.
runners.comment: Inconsistent this winter and he looks vulnerable on a rare 6f run

runners.name: Bernie The Bear
runners.spotlight: Still seeking his first win for Linda Perratt but he has largely been in fair form of late and won't mind conditions; step back up to 7f backfired here yesterday.
runners.comment: On a long losing run but he has largely been in fair form of late

runners.name: Big Time Rascal
runners.spotlight: Has a chance on last season's best, notably a 6f win at Brighton (RPR 59); ended that campaign quietly though and he has a 176-day absence to overcome.
runners.comment: 6f Brighton win last May but ended the campaign quietly and absent for 176 days

races.name: Midnite: Built For 2026 Not 2006 Handicap

runners.name: American Bay
runners.spotlight: He was returning from a 167-day break when third over C&D two weeks ago; Billy Loughnane retains the ride and he is one to take seriously.
runners.comment: Good third over C&D two weeks ago (after a break); Billy Loughnane retains the ride

runners.name: Beaumadier
runners.spotlight: He has two impressive C&D wins on his record this winter; not at his best on his last two starts but he's not an easy horse to pass if breaking on terms; needs another personal best but it is not out of the question.
runners.comment: Two impressive C&D wins this winter; should go well if breaking on terms

runners.name: Counsel
runners.spotlight: C&D winner last February; back to winning ways with a front-running 6f win at Lingfield in December; not quite so good twice since and he needs the 11-week break to have freshened him up.
runners.comment: C&D winner; not at best on last two starts and needs to be revived by his 11-week break

runners.name: Storm Call
runners.spotlight: Poorly drawn when down the field over C&D when last seen in October; prior to that she had won well in first-time blinkers at Redcar and she remains of interest from a handicapping perspective.
runners.comment: Poor draw here when last seen; successful on turf one week earlier; unexposed in blinkers

runners.name: Smooth Silesie
runners.spotlight: Won four turf handicaps in the second half of 2025, including a ready win at Yarmouth when last seen in October; up 5lb, raised in class and returning from 162 days off so it is easy enough to look elsewhere.
runners.comment: Four turf wins in the 2nd half of 2025; up in class for reappearance; others are stronger

runners.name: Mr Lightside
runners.spotlight: Recorded an RPR of 103 on his seasonal return last April but the wheels have come off since; went off quickly and didn't get home in a 6f handicap at Kempton three weeks ago (blinkered first time); returns to 5f and things might click again soon.
runners.comment: Disappointing after his promising return last April; return to 5f a plus; dangerous mark

races.name: Midnite Aint Your Grandads Bookie Novice Stakes (GBB Race)

runners.name: Merlier
runners.spotlight: Beaten a nose at Newcastle (5f) on last month's debut and comfortably went one better over the same C&D 13 days ago; open to further progress and he rates a solid contender.
runners.comment: Beaten a nose on debut before an easy win 13 days ago (both 5f, Newcastle); more to come

runners.name: Time To Take Off
runners.spotlight: Travelled powerfully and won with more authority than the half-length winning margin might suggest on her 2yo debut at Nottingham in October (5f, good to soft; form franked); she looks a useful prospect and is greatly respected back from 173 days off.
runners.comment: Impressed at Nottingham on only 2yo start (5f, good to soft); looks a good prospect

runners.name: Burdett Estate
runners.spotlight: Third of five over C&D on debut in November (the winner of that race had earlier been well beaten by Time To Take Off); big step forward when winning back over C&D one month later (made all); that race has thrown up winners but the other penalised runners may have stronger form.
runners.comment: Two C&D runs in late 2025, 3rd on debut before ready front-running win; this demands more

races.name: Win Â£250,000 With BetMGM's Golden Goals Handicap

runners.name: Mr Nugget
runners.spotlight: Another who is enjoying a fine winter, winning for the fifth time when seeing off ten rivals at Southwell last month; steadily-run race no good to him at Newcastle on Saturday and he's capable of bouncing back quickly.
runners.comment: Five wins this winter, including C&D; not suited by steady tempo at Newcastle on Saturday

runners.name: Farasi Lane
runners.spotlight: Getting on for two years since he last won but he is operating off a reduced mark these days and he ran well over C&D in both December and January; not the easiest ride and Rossa Ryan replaces a 7lb claimer today; should go well.
runners.comment: Tricky customer on a long losing run; dangerous mark though and Rossa Ryan booked tonight

runners.name: Francesi
runners.spotlight: Sits third, one place behind Aisling Oscar, in the All Weather Horse of the Year standings after his fine winter; yet to win at Wolverhampton but he has run well here; Billy Loughnane takes over tonight; each-way shout.
runners.comment: Had fine winter but his wins have come at other tracks; e-w shout with B Loughnane booked

runners.name: Helm Rock
runners.spotlight: Nine-time winner, the latest a Class 4 at Haydock last June off just 1lb lower; satisfactory return from a six-month break 20 days ago; this is less demanding and he is one to consider.
runners.comment: Didn't run badly on his recent return; down in grade and should have a big run in him

runners.name: Panama Black
runners.spotlight: Just one win to his name from 16 attempts but he has run well in two of his three starts this year, including C&D; that said, he wouldn't appear to have much in hand of his mark.
runners.comment: Poor strike-rate but he has turned in two good efforts from three starts this year

runners.name: Mollie Foster
runners.spotlight: Won twice for Ed Walker, including on Tapeta (1m), but she has finished down the field in two runs for this yard (in season latest); has a point to prove for now.
runners.comment: Not found her best in two runs for current stable; has a point to prove for now

runners.name: Spaceport
runners.spotlight: Won turf handicaps over 1m4f and 1m6f for William Muir/Chris Grassick in 2024; only seen twice for this stable and finished tailed off on both occasions (hurdles last February/1m4f here last month); hooded and dropped in trip today; risky despite a tumbling handicap mark.
runners.comment: Two runs in 13 months for this yard and tailed off both times (hurdles/1m4f); now hooded

races.name: Prix Bango (Grade 3 AQPS) (Turf)

runners.name: Lili Star
runners.owner: Mme Veronique Van Den Broele & Norbert Leenders

runners.name: Matcha De LIsle
runners.owner: Mme Giada Menato & Franco Alberto Menato

runners.name: Makani Du Fenoir
runners.owner: Domaine Du Fenoir F Bellemere & Mme P Dotte-Charvy
